creator: thomas@gibfest.dk
text:
ok so today it started acting up again:

------------------
[tykling@nuc1 ~]$ time sysctl dev.hwpstate_intel

user    0m0.000s

[tykling@nuc1 ~]$ 
------------------

I ran procstat -kka while it was hanging and grepped for the pid in the output:

------------------
[tykling@nuc1 ~]$ sudo procstat -kka > procstat.txt
[tykling@nuc1 ~]$ grep 38907 procstat.txt 
38907 112055 sysctl              -                   mi_switch+0xbf sched_bind+0x72 sysctl_epp_select+0xb3 sysctl_root_handler_locked+0x91 sysctl_root+0x244 userland_sysctl+0x176 sys___sysctl+0x60 amd64_syscall+0x110 fast_syscall_common+0xf8 
[tykling@nuc1 ~]$ 
------------------

Was this what you were looking for? I have the rest of procstat.txt still but I have to reboot the host now because it is affecting operation. Slowly more and more alerts for weird network related stuff starts triggering until eventually I cannot reach it anymore over the network.

Thanks!
